summary: chain-key-validator ships wallet/credential stealer with hardcoded C2
details: The package contains multiple modules (build-verifier.js, scanner.js, wallet-scanner.js) that combine system information collection (os.hostname, username, cwd), filesystem enumeration (fs.existsSync against home directory paths), child_process execution (execSync, including 'docker push' and 'whoami'), and outbound HTTPS POST requests. scanner.js hardcodes the exfiltration endpoint https://ddjidd564.github.io (a personal GitHub Pages site, not consistent with a 'chain-key-validator' library's stated purpose). wallet-scanner.js is a >1000-line module performing wallet enumeration and POSTing collected data over HTTPS, with curl/ping primitives present. The presence of a dedicated wallet-scanner module combined with hardcoded C2 to a throwaway-shaped GitHub Pages account is the credential/wallet-stealer fingerprint. The package name suggests a generic blockchain validation utility but the shipped code is a host enumeration and wallet exfiltration toolkit.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
